name: Mexican Lasagna- Altered
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups salsa (Old El Paso Thick 'N Chunky), plus 1/2 cup
1/2 to 1 tsp Taco House seasoning salt
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown ground beef in a nonstick skillet or over olive oil until well-drained.
2. Add chopped onion and continue cooking until translucent.
3. Remove from heat and stir in refried beans, salsa, oregano, cumin, and garlic powder.
4. Place 4 noodles at the bottom of a 13x9x2 inch baking pan coated with nonstick spray or lightly greased.
5. Combine 1 1/2 cups Monterey jack cheese with 1 1/2 cups Cheddar cheese.
6. Spread one-third of the meat mixture over the noodles, then top with the Jack/Cheddar cheese mix.
7. Continue layering with remaining ingredients, finishing with a layer of noodles.
8. Cover pan with foil and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
9. In a separate bowl, combine sour cream, sliced green onions, black olives, Taco House seasoning, half of the remaining Monterey Jack cheese, and half cup salsa.
10. Remove from oven and spread the sour cream mixture evenly over the lasagna.
11. Sprinkle with remaining cheese and return to oven uncovered for 15 to 20 minutes, or until cheese is melted.
